things.name: Paisley Abbey
things.description: Magnificent medieval abbey founded in 1163 — burial place of Robert the Bruce's mother and the historical heart of the town

things.name: Paisley Museum (Newly Redeveloped)
things.description: Transformed world-class museum celebrating Paisley's unique pattern heritage and its status as 2021 UK City of Culture candidate

things.name: Coats Observatory
things.description: Scotland's oldest continuously operating public observatory (1882) with a remarkable telescope and space weather station open to visitors

things.name: Sma' Shot Cottages
things.description: Restored 18th-century artisan weavers' cottages telling the story of the Paisley weavers and the Sma' Shot Day tradition

things.name: Thomas Coats Memorial Church
things.description: Stunning Victorian Baptist church described as the 'Cathedral of Nonconformity' — one of Scotland's finest 19th-century church buildings

things.name: Gleniffer Braes Country Park
things.description: Moorland country park above Paisley with sweeping views over the Clyde valley and a network of walking trails through heather

things.name: Elderslie (William Wallace Birthplace Monument)
things.description: Traditional birthplace of Sir William Wallace, Scotland's national hero — a small park and monument in the village of Elderslie

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's largest city just 20 minutes by train — world-class museums, galleries, Mackintosh architecture and vibrant West End

things.name: Barshaw Park
things.description: Beloved Victorian public park with a boating pond, model railway, nature centre and tranquil walks along the White Cart Water

things.name: Glasgow Airport Observation (Spotting)
things.description: Glasgow Airport's terminal is among the busiest in Scotland — watch aircraft from the viewing areas or explore the aviation heritage
